title: SICS Human-State Proxy Benchmark Event Markers Schema v0.1
description: Schema for validating one row object from events.csv in the SICS Human-State Proxy Benchmark Track synthetic/sample package. This schema is for public helper use only. It does not define CAIS, does not define Sal-Meter, does not grant compliance, does not validate benchmark performance, and does not create diagnostic, therapeutic, clinical, surveillance, employment, insurance, educational, legal, or eligibility authority.

event_id.description: Unique event marker identifier within the synthetic/sample session.

event_type.description: Synthetic or research-stage event marker type. These are timing markers only and are not diagnostic labels.

event_timestamp.description: Event timestamp in ISO 8601 UTC-like format for the synthetic session.

event_source.description: Source that generated or recorded the event marker.

event_description.description: Human-readable event description. Must not contain participant identifiers, clinical interpretation, Sal-Meter validation claims, or CAIS compliance claims.

operator_confirmed.description: Whether the event marker was operator-confirmed. CSV readers may load this as a string.

condition_label.description: Optional non-diagnostic condition marker associated with the event.

raw_human_data_present.description: Optional explicit declaration. For public sample data this must be false.

identifiable_data_present.description: Optional explicit declaration. For public sample data this must be false.

clinical_data_present.description: Optional explicit declaration. For public sample data this must be false.

sal_meter_input_present.description: Optional explicit declaration. For current proxy sample data this must be false.

cais_compliance_claim_present.description: Optional explicit declaration. For public helper files this must be false.

notes.description: Optional boundary note. Must not contain participant identifiers, clinical interpretation, Sal-Meter validation claims, or CAIS compliance claims.
